steeringCommand: I want to not only use the git history but the agentviz session and history
whatHappened: Merged git and session data into a single chronological timeline. Both sources tagged with badges. Commit: 1db4986
levelUp: 🔀 **Dual-source view.** Git history and session events in one timeline.

steeringCommand: Submit a test and evals to go with this
whatHappened: 42 tests written across 3 suites. Tests caught 2 real bugs: milestone threshold and timezone sort. Eval baselines in docs/evals.md. Commit: 248b0e4
levelUp: ✅ **Test suite built.** 42 tests, 2 bugs caught before shipping.

steeringCommand: Are the steering commands actual prompts?
whatHappened: Honesty check exposed that git entries are commit messages, not prompts. Added italic quotes for real prompts, plain text for commits. Source badges added. Commit: 44bee71
levelUp: ✍️ **Honest visual design.** Real prompts quoted, commits plain — the difference is visible.

steeringCommand: Introduced a bug. AGENTVIZ crashed. GIT_COLORS is not defined
whatHappened: Three stale variable references missed during rename. Fixed, then added 6 JSDOM component render tests that catch this class of error. Commits: 36a00e2, 7c968c0
levelUp: 🔧 **Failure drove testing.** Production crash led to component render tests that prevent it.

steeringCommand: I dont want to have to explicitly contribute. I want it built in
whatHappened: Added auto-contribute: session steering persists to .agentviz/steering-v1.jsonl on load. Redaction strips secrets. 200-entry retention with versioned rotation. Commit: fd382d8
levelUp: 🤖 **Zero-friction persistence.** Steering saved automatically, secrets redacted.

steeringCommand: Is there a better word than Journal? Tell me five suggestions
whatHappened: Evaluated Story, Steering, Arc, Log, Narrative. "Steering" won — names the thing directly. Renamed across all UI, tests, docs. Commit: b414d29
levelUp: 🏷️ **Naming as design.** Steering says what the view IS, not what it feels like.

steeringCommand: I do not see any level up after the steering. It does not show the causal level up
whatHappened: Added temporal matching: each steering entry finds the commit it produced. Used reverse-chronological search for contributed entries (persisted after the commit). Commit: ee9b3bb
levelUp: 🔗 **Cause and effect.** Steering linked to the commit it produced.

steeringCommand: The taste and quality is not close to what I want. Look at git-for-pms as the exemplar
whatHappened: Studied the git-for-pms journal. Rewrote both level-up synthesizers to use emoji + bold headline + insight sentence format. Tightened steering column to 110 chars. Commit: 2acf82c
levelUp: 🎨 **Exemplar quality adopted.** Level-ups now follow the Scribe format from git-for-pms.

steeringCommand: After the data is projected, do another pass to understand the most impactful steering commands
whatHappened: Added impact scoring: linesChanged from git shortstat + turns driven per steering. Impact bars show proportional size. Commit: f03f856
levelUp: 📊 **Impact made visible.** Lines changed + turns driven = steering weight.

steeringCommand: Can the squad reasoning be included in what happened details?
whatHappened: Extractor now collects all assistant response chunks per turn (up to 6000 chars), filtering out tool invocations. Detail panel shows full Squad Response with scroll.
levelUp: 🧠 **Squad reasoning captured.** Agent perspectives visible in the detail panel.
